title: 11 Herbal Creams For Periodontal Disease
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a helpful addition to your oral health routine, especially when it comes to managing periodontal disease. Periodontal disease is an infection of the gums that can cause them to become inflamed and even lead to tooth loss. Herbal teas have been shown to help relieve periodontal disease by reducing inflammation and killing bacteria in the mouth. For example, Curcuma longa, or turmeric, contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically as a cream, turmeric can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums.  Another example is Salvia officinalis, or sage, which has been shown to have antibacterial properties that can help kill bacteria in the mouth and prevent the spread of periodontal disease. Eucalyptus globulus, or eucalyptus, has decongestant properties that can help reduce inflammation and ease pain in the gums. When used in a cream, these herbs can be absorbed into the skin and work directly on the infected area to provide relief. Using herbal creams can be a natural and non-invasive way to manage periodontal disease, and can be especially helpful for people who are sensitive to medications or prefer a more holistic approach to their oral health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains a compound called curcumin, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. Curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in reducing inflammation and killing bacteria that cause periodontal disease. The curcumin in Curcuma longa creams also has antioxidant properties, which help protect the gums and teeth from damage caused by free radicals. Another compound found in Curcuma longa creams is demethoxycurcumin (DMC), which has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to periodontal disease. By targeting the underlying causes of periodontal disease, Curcuma longa creams can help prevent further damage and promote healthy gums and teeth.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for periodontal disease
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder, and 2 tablespoons of essential oil in a double boiler.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in the double boiler over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Add the turmeric powder and stir until it's fully incorporated into the melted mixture.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool for 10-15 minutes, or until it reaches a consistency similar to thin honey.
Pour the mixture into a small container and add the essential oil. Mix well and let the Curcuma longa cream cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams contains rosmarinic acid, apigenin, and luteolin as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and prevent further damage to the gums. Rosmarinic acid, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis. Luteolin also has antimicrobial properties, which help combat the infection and promote healing of the gums. By reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth, Salvia officinalis creams can help manage periodontal disease and promote a healthier oral environment.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for periodontal disease
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Salvia officinalis leaves, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and a double boiler.
Heat the coconut oil in the double boiler until it reaches 180°F (82°C).
Add the dried Salvia officinalis leaves to the coconut oil and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container. Discard the solids. Add the beeswax and vitamin E oil, and stir until the beeswax is melted.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store the creams in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains essential oils rich in eucalyptol, a compound that has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil also contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The antibacterial properties of eucalyptol help to reduce the growth of bacteria that can cause periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis. Eucalyptol's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums, while flavonoids can help to reduce oxidative stress and promote healing. The combination of these properties makes eucalyptus globulus creams a potential treatment option for periodontal disease.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for periodontal disease
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of eucalyptus globulus essential oil, and a double boiler.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in the double boiler over low heat.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and add the eucalyptus globulus essential oil. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify.
Once solid, label and store the eucalyptus globulus cream in a cool place. Use 1-2 teaspoons as needed for periodontal care.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams contains bioactive constituents such as cineole, limonene, and alpha-terpineol. These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which help combat the bacteria that cause periodontal disease. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of these bioactive constituents help reduce swelling and kill bacteria in the gums, promoting healthy gum tissue. The tea tree oil in Melaleuca alternifolia creams also has antifungal properties, which can help prevent fungal infections in the gums. This helps maintain the balance of bacteria in the mouth and prevents further gum disease.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for periodontal disease
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of distilled water, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Heat the distilled water in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in the double boiler or heat-proof bowl. Stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Remove the mixture from the heat and stir in the Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil and vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into a heat-proof container and let it cool. Once cool, whip the mixture with a hand mixer or electric mixer to create a smooth, creamy texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active constituents like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the gums, making them less prone to infection and disease. The antimicrobial properties of gingerol and shogaol also help prevent the formation of plaque, a major contributor to periodontal disease. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of zingiber officinale help protect the gums from oxidative stress, promoting healthy gum tissue. By reducing inflammation, killing bacteria, and protecting gums from oxidative stress, zingiber officinale creams can help manage periodontal disease.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for periodontal disease
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of grated Zingiber officinale (ginger) root, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the grated ginger root to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture. Stir well to combine.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly. Add the distilled water and vitamin E oil. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify completely. Once solid, the Zingiber officinale cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains bioactive constituents like alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the gums, a common symptom of periodontal disease. The glycosides in Echinacea purpurea creams also have antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria, which contribute to the development of periodontal disease. Additionally, the phenolic acids in Echinacea purpurea creams have antioxidant properties that help protect the gums from oxidative damage and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and inhibiting bacterial growth, Echinacea purpurea creams may help alleviate symptoms of periodontal disease, promoting healthier gums and teeth.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for periodontal disease
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of lavender oil.
Combine dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and coconut oil in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat for 2-3 hours, or until the oil turns green.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Discard the solids.
In a separate saucepan, melt the beeswax over low heat. Add the strained oil and vitamin E oil. Stir until well combined.
Remove the saucepan from heat and add lavender oil. Pour the mixture into small containers. Let it cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, bilobalide, and ginkgolides. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are beneficial in treating periodontal disease. Flavonoids, in particular, help reduce inflammation and prevent the formation of plaque, a key contributor to periodontal disease. Bilobalide has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce oxidative stress in the gums, promoting healing and reducing the risk of gum recession. By inhibiting platelet-activating factor, ginkgolides also prevent the formation of blood clots and reduce inflammation in the gums, further aiding in the treatment of periodontal disease.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for periodontal disease
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract, 10 drops of tea tree oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments until smooth.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Add Ginkgo biloba extract and tea tree oil to the mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let cool. Use a spoon to create a smooth, even texture. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus creams contains a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including limonene, linalool, and geraniol. These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the growth of bacteria and inflammation in the gums, common symptoms of periodontal disease. The geraniol in Cymbopogon citratus creams also has antioxidant properties, which help to protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of the limonene and linalool help to prevent the formation of plaque, a major contributor to periodontal disease. By reducing inflammation, killing bacteria, and preventing plaque formation, Cymbopogon citratus creams helps to support healthy gums and prevent the progression of periodontal disease.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal creams for periodontal disease
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Cymbopogon citratus essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir until beeswax is fully dissolved.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and add Cymbopogon citratus essential oil and vitamin E oil. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature. This may take about 30 minutes.
Once solidified, your Cymbopogon citratus cream is ready to use. Apply it to the affected gum area 2-3 times a day to help manage periodontal disease.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams contains thymol and carvacrol, two active compounds with antimicrobial properties. These compounds help kill bacteria that cause periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis. Thymol's ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and carvacrol's capacity to reduce inflammation make it an effective treatment for periodontal pockets and gum inflammation. The antiseptic properties of Thymus vulgaris creams also help prevent the formation of plaque and tartar on teeth, further reducing the risk of periodontal disease. By targeting the root causes of periodontal disease, Thymus vulgaris creams can help reduce symptoms and promote overall oral health.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for periodontal disease
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of olive oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of Thymus vulgaris essential oil.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the olive oil and distilled water to the melted beeswax, and stir until well combined.
Remove the mixture from the heat and stir in the Thymus vulgaris essential oil.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify before using as a cream for periodontal disease treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains rosmarinic acid, a potent antioxidant, and carnosic acid, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties. These constituents help reduce inflammation in the gums and prevent the progression of periodontal disease. The antimicrobial properties of rosmarinic acid also inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections and disease. Additionally, carnosic acid has been shown to reduce oxidative stress and protect against tissue damage. By reducing inflammation and inhibiting bacterial growth, Rosmarinus officinalis creams can help alleviate symptoms of periodontal disease and promote oral health.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for periodontal disease
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of rosemary essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments until melted.
Add the rosemary essential oil and vitamin E oil to the melted mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar or tin and let it cool until solidified. This will take about 30 minutes.
Once solid, your Rosmarinus officinalis cream is ready to use. Apply a small amount to the affected area 2-3 times a day to help soothe and prevent periodontal disease.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum creams contains cinnamaldehyde, a key active constituent that has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to combat periodontal disease by reducing the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth and decreasing inflammation in the gums. Cinnamaldehyde also has antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the gums and teeth from damage caused by free radicals. The creams' antiseptic properties help to prevent infection and promote healing of the gums, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and discomfort. The combination of these properties in Cinnamomum verum creams makes it a potential treatment option for periodontal disease.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal creams for periodontal disease
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Cinnamomum verum oil, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Heat the Cinnamomum verum oil and coconut oil in a saucepan over low heat until melted.
Add 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil and 1 teaspoon of honey to the melted oil mixture.
Remove the saucepan from heat and add 2 tablespoons of beeswax. Stir until the beeswax is fully melted.
Pour the cream mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify before use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent periodontal disease. Some teas, like peppermint and sage, contain compounds that reduce bacteria in the mouth. This can lower the risk of infection and inflammation that lead to gum disease. Regular tea consumption might also improve gum health by reducing plaque and promoting a balanced oral environment.
faq_safe: While herbal teas may help with periodontal disease, consuming them daily isn't risk-free. Some teas, like sage and chamomile, can thin the blood and affect medications. Also, strong brews can irritate gums. Moderate use is best, but be cautious and listen to your body.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with periodontal disease, but it's hard to predict exactly when you'll see results. Some people notice a difference in a few weeks, while others may not see improvements until after a few months. It really depends on the individual and the severity of the disease.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for periodontal disease in the morning or before meals can be helpful. This is because it may help reduce bacteria in the mouth and ease inflammation. It's best to drink it after brushing your teeth, so you can feel its benefits in the areas you need it most.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for periodontal disease. Some teas, like those with blood-thinning properties, may increase the risk of bleeding during dental procedures. Others, like those with liver-boosting properties, may change how your body processes antibiotics or other medications. Be cautious when combining herbal teas with prescription medications.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas for periodontal disease. Some herbs like sage and chamomile are usually safe, but others like parsley and dandelion root might not be. These teas can affect the baby's development or cause bleeding issues during pregnancy. It's better to choose gentle, well-known options and check labels carefully.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas may be safe to consume while breastfeeding, but it's essential to check the ingredients. Some herbal teas, like sage and parsley, have been studied for their potential benefits in reducing gum inflammation. However, it's crucial to avoid teas that may interact with breastfeeding or affect milk supply, like peppermint and ginger.
title_2: Combat Periodontal Disease with 11 Natural Herbal Creams for Aging
